winner: binary funnel (rotation x2 + residual) + tiling, served by carousel
note: Two opposite Pareto frontiers: recall-per-QPS -> binary funnel wins outright (10-15x PQ QPS at equal recall); recall-per-byte -> PQ/OPQ win (32B@0.99 vs 128B) but only matter when RAM-bound. The deciding physics: POPCOUNT (VPOPCNTDQ, vectorized) beats GATHER (PQ/OPQ ADC lookups, not vectorized) - so 1-bit+popcount is throughput-optimal on CPU; fewer bytes help footprint not speed (011/050/054 confirmed). PQ/ITQ/OPQ exploration CONFIRMS the binary funnel as the QPS winner rather than beating it. Best stacked: ~0.995 recall @ ~960 QPS @ 8ms, no-cliff serving to ~940 QPS, 128MB, one 8-core box.
